name: mailvec
description: Local hybrid (FTS5 + vector) search over your Maildir email archive.
long_description: Mailvec exposes the local Mailvec archive (built by the indexer/embedder background services) over MCP. Provides search_emails (keyword, semantic, or hybrid), get_email, get_thread, list_folders, view_attachment, get_attachment_text, and get_attachment_page_image against a SQLite database with sqlite-vec embeddings. The bundled binary is the read-side stdio server only; the indexer and embedder are run separately as your own services. Database path, Maildir root, Ollama endpoint, and Fastmail account id are NOT configured here — they live in ~/Library/Application Support/Mailvec/appsettings.Local.json (written by ops/install.sh) so a single source of truth is shared with the launchd-installed services and the CLI. Attachment reads are fully in-memory and write nothing to disk: view_attachment shows an image (Claude vision) or a small text file inline, get_attachment_text returns the extracted text of a document (PDF, DOCX, etc.), and get_attachment_page_image renders a PDF page to an inline image for tables, forms, or scanned pages. To save an attachment as a file, use the Mailvec tray app's Save button or `mailvec extract-attachments`.

log_tool_calls.title: Log tool calls (debug)
log_tool_calls.description: When on, the server logs each tool invocation (args + result summary) to ~/Library/Logs/Claude/mcp-server-mailvec.log. Useful for picking real Claude examples to iterate on result quality. Leave off in normal use.

tools.name: search_emails
tools.description: Search the archive by keyword, semantic similarity, or hybrid RRF fusion. Supports folder, date, sender, and attachment filters (hasAttachments, attachmentType e.g. 'pdf'/'image').

tools.name: get_email
tools.description: Fetch a single message by its message_id header or internal id. Returns body text and per-attachment metadata.

tools.name: get_thread
tools.description: Fetch all messages in a thread given any message in the thread. Each entry lists its attachments (partIndex, filename, extraction status) so attachment tools can be called directly.

tools.name: list_folders
tools.description: List Maildir folders with message counts.

tools.name: view_attachment
tools.description: Show an email attachment inline, in memory — nothing is written to disk. Images come back as an image block (Claude vision; large or non-JPEG/PNG/GIF/WebP images are automatically downscaled/re-encoded to JPEG); small text-ish files as decoded UTF-8 text. Other binary types (PDF, DOCX, zip) return a summary pointing at get_attachment_text / get_attachment_page_image.

tools.name: get_attachment_text
tools.description: Return the plain text Mailvec already extracted from an attachment (PDF, DOCX, etc.), straight from the database — no filesystem needed, so it works over a remote connection. Long documents are paged via maxChars/offset (default 50,000 chars per call). Use this to read or summarise a document's contents; falls back to a clear message for scanned/encrypted/unsupported attachments.

tools.name: get_attachment_page_image
tools.description: Render one page of a PDF attachment to a JPEG image (returned inline so Claude can see it). Use when layout matters or text extraction is empty: tables, charts, forms, signatures, or scanned/image-only PDFs. One page per call (1-based page param).
